title: Samosa Crispy Potato Pastry

seo.title: Authentic Crispy Potato Samosa Recipe | Indian Appetizer
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Crispy Potato Samosa recipe at home! This authentic Indian appetizer features a flavorful potato filling in a perfectly crisp pastry. Get step-by-step instructions.
longTailKeywords: how to make Crispy Potato Samosa
Crispy Potato Samosa step by step
best Crispy Potato Samosa at home
seo.h1: Authentic Crispy Potato Samosa Recipe: Your Guide to Indian Appetizers
h2Headings: What is a Crispy Potato Samosa?
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Crispy Potato Samosa
Pro Tips for Perfectly Crispy Samosas
seo.imageAlt: A plate of golden-brown, crispy potato samosas with mint chutney, ready to be served as an Indian appetizer.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy these homemade crispy potato samosas, a beloved Indian snack perfect for any occasion!

description: Indulge in the iconic flavors of India with these irresistible crispy potato samosas. Each golden-brown pastry encases a perfectly spiced, savory potato and pea filling, offering a delightful crunch with every bite. They are the ultimate comfort food and a fantastic appetizer for any gathering.

instructions.title: Prepare the Samosa Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, salt, and carom seeds. Add melted ghee or vegetable oil and rub it into the flour with your fingertips until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add cold water, a little at a time, and knead to form a firm, smooth dough. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for at least 20 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Potato Filling
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, and chopped onion (if using), and saute until the onion turns translucent. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and red chili powder, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine Filling Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the coarsely mashed potatoes and green peas to the pan. Mix well, ensuring the spices are evenly distributed. Stir in garam masala, dry mango powder, and salt to taste. Cook for 2-3 minutes, gently mashing some of the potatoes to bind the filling. Remove from heat and stir in fresh coriander leaves. Let the filling cool completely.

instructions.title: Shape the Samosas
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into 8-10 equal portions. Roll each portion into an oval shape, about 6-7 inches long and 3-4 inches wide. Cut the oval in half lengthwise to create two semi-circles. Take one semi-circle, moisten the straight edge with water, and bring the two ends together to form a cone. Press firmly to seal the seam.

instructions.title: Fill and Seal Samosas
instructions.text: Spoon about 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled potato filling into the prepared cone. Moisten the top edges of the cone with water. Bring the two open edges together, pinching them to seal the samosa completely, creating the characteristic triangular shape. Ensure there are no gaps for oil to enter. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.

instructions.title: Deep Fry the Samosas
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil for deep frying in a deep pan or wok over medium-low heat. The oil should not be too hot, as this will prevent the samosas from cooking through and becoming crispy. Gently slide a few samosas into the oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry them on low to medium heat, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides, which takes about 10-12 minutes per batch.

instructions.title: Drain and Serve
instructions.text: Once golden and crispy, carefully remove the samosas from the oil using a slotted spoon. Place them on a wire rack or paper towels to drain excess oil. Serve hot with your favorite chutney, such as mint chutney or tamarind chutney, and a hot cup of chai.

tips: For truly crispy samosas, ensure the dough is firm and kneaded well, and fry them on medium-low heat to allow even cooking and browning.
Adjust the spice level by varying the amount of green chilies and red chili powder. For a tangier flavor, a pinch more amchur can be added to the filling.

Samosas are best served hot and fresh. You can prepare the filling and dough ahead of time, then assemble and fry just before serving. Leftover fried samosas can be reheated in an oven or air fryer for crispness.
article: Samosa Crispy Potato Pastry is a beloved Indian Appetizers dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
